articles.title: Trump Urges Restraint as Iran-Israel Conflict Escalates to Day 101
bullets: Iran fired a wave of missiles at northern Israel after Israel attacked Beirut's Dahiyeh suburb.
President Trump urged Netanyahu not to retaliate, warning it could derail ongoing Iran peace talks.
Israel launched strikes on Tehran, Tabriz, and Isfahan; Trump told FT he 'calls all the shots.'

articles.title: Gunfire at Ohio Street Festival Wounds at Least 12 People
bullets: Shots erupted Saturday near a busy Ohio street festival, wounding at least 12 people.
No fatalities reported; several victims were transported to area hospitals.
Police are investigating; suspect believed to have fled the scene after the shooting.

articles.title: May Jobs Report: US Employers Add Surprising 172,000 Positions
bullets: Nonfarm payrolls rose by 172,000 in May, roughly double analyst consensus forecasts.
Unemployment remained at 4.3%; strong data pushed Federal Reserve rate-hike odds higher.
Stock markets slid as yields rose sharply, with the Nasdaq suffering its worst day since April 2025.

articles.title: 2026 Measles Surge: 30 New Outbreaks as US Reviews Elimination Status
bullets: The US has recorded 30 new measles outbreaks in 2026, threatening the nation's elimination status.
Officials are preparing a formal review of elimination status at the CDC.
Health authorities urge vaccination as the outbreak is the worst in recent years.

articles.title: Tony Awards 2026: 'Schmigadoon!' Wins Best Musical, 'Liberation' Best Play
bullets: 'Schmigadoon!' took home the Tony Award for Best Musical at the 2026 ceremony.
'Liberation' won Best Play, capping a strong comeback year for Broadway.
The Tony Awards recognized a record number of productions this season.

articles.title: Iran-Israel War Day 101: Missiles Fly as New Full-Scale War Fears Rise
bullets: Israel struck Tehran, Tabriz, and Isfahan; Iran responded with a second wave of missiles at northern Israel.
Explosions rocked Iran's capital and major cities following Israeli air attacks over the weekend.
Trump called both leaders urging a stop to hostilities; ceasefire negotiations remain fragile.

articles.title: Zelenskyy Meets Starmer, Macron, Merz in London Over Ukraine Air Defenses
bullets: Leaders discussed the 'urgent need to scale up' Ukraine's air defenses and deep-strike capabilities.
Russia fired hypersonic Oreshnik weapons at Ukraine; a drone hit near the Chornobyl nuclear site.
Zelenskyy warned Ukraine is 'very close' to producing its own ballistic missile to strike Russia.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV Presides Over Corpus Christi Mass in Spain — First Papal Visit in 15 Years
bullets: Pope Leo XIV led a Corpus Christi Mass in Madrid during the first papal visit to Spain in 15 years.
The visit drew large crowds and significant international media attention.
The new pope has made outreach to Europe a cornerstone of his early pontificate.

articles.title: PEPFAR Funding Cuts Already Costing Lives in South Africa and Mozambique
bullets: Health care providers say US PEPFAR funding cancellations under Trump are endangering vulnerable patients.
Redirected funding has forced clinic closures and supply shortages in two African nations.
Aid groups warn the cuts could reverse decades of progress against HIV/AIDS in sub-Saharan Africa.

articles.title: Peru Presidential Election: Tight Race Between Fujimori and Leftist Sanchez
bullets: Polls suggest a polarized but close race between hard-right Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sanchez.
The election could reshape Peru's relations with the US and China amid ongoing economic turbulence.
Voter turnout is expected to be high despite widespread distrust of both leading candidates.

articles.title: Delhi Heat Wave: Heat Index Hits 45.1°C; Monsoon Expected June 25-30
bullets: Delhi's heat index breached 45.1°C Sunday despite an actual max temperature of 41.6°C.
IMD forecasts light rainfall with winds up to 60 kmph on June 11-12 for the region.
Southwest monsoon is expected to reach Delhi-NCR between June 25 and June 30.

articles.title: India Faces 50% US Tariffs Over Russian Crude Purchases and Stalled Trade Deal
bullets: Trump imposed 50% tariffs on India after New Delhi continued purchasing Russian crude oil.
A bilateral trade deal failed to meet its agreed deadline, triggering the tariff escalation.
Modi government faces hard choices as foreign policy setbacks compound domestic pressures.

articles.title: ZEE Secures India Media Rights for 39 FIFA Events Through 2034 Including World Cup
bullets: ZEE Entertainment won exclusive India media rights for 39 FIFA events from 2026 through 2034.
Rights package includes FIFA World Cup 2026, FIFA World Cup 2030, and other major tournaments.
The deal marks a major victory for ZEE as streaming competition in India intensifies.

articles.title: India Remains World's Fastest-Growing Major Economy With 8.2% GDP Growth
bullets: India's economy grew 8.2% between July and September 2025, outpacing all other major economies.
Modi government points to GST reforms, new labour codes, and free trade deals with UK and Oman.
US tariffs and geopolitical headwinds pose the key risks to India's 2026 growth outlook.

articles.title: India to Host BRICS Summit and Quad Summit in 2026 Amid Global Tensions
bullets: India is scheduled to host both the BRICS Summit and the Quad Summit in 2026.
The back-to-back summits position India as a key diplomatic hub amid US-China rivalry.
Earlier this year India hosted the AI Impact Summit in February, drawing global tech leaders.

articles.title: Nasdaq Drops 4.2% Friday — Worst Session Since April 2025 on Jobs Shock
bullets: The Nasdaq fell 4.2% on June 6, its worst single-day drop since April 2025.
The S&P 500 lost 2.6% and the Dow shed 1.4% in a broad selloff driven by semiconductor weakness.
Strong May jobs data (172,000 new hires) erased rate-cut hopes and rattled markets.

articles.title: Marvell Technology Surges 9% Premarket as New S&P 500 Addition Announced
bullets: Marvell Technology (MRVL) jumped 9% to $287.05 in premarket after joining the S&P 500.
Flex (FLEX) also rose 4.3% to $158.50 on its own S&P 500 inclusion announcement.
Index additions typically boost stocks as passive funds must buy shares to track the index.

articles.title: Crude Oil Prices Surge as Iran-Israel Strikes Escalate Middle East Tensions
bullets: Crude oil spiked sharply after Iran fired missiles at Israel and Israel struck Iranian cities.
Energy analysts warn sustained Middle East conflict could push Brent above key resistance levels.
The Dow is poised to weaken while the Nasdaq may rise as geopolitical risk reshapes sector rotation.

articles.title: South Korea KOSPI Plunges 9% on Monday, Circuit Breakers Triggered
bullets: South Korea's KOSPI index fell nearly 9% on Monday, triggering automatic circuit breakers.
The selloff was driven by US jobs data fueling Fed rate-hike fears, hitting tech stocks hardest.
Asian markets broadly fell as investors reassessed risk following Friday's US market rout.

articles.title: Rate Hike Odds Climb After Surprise May Payrolls Beat; Fed Watch Intensifies
bullets: May's 172,000 payroll gain, double the forecast, caused rate-hike probability to climb sharply.
Yields rose as investors repriced the Fed path, unwinding previous rate-cut bets.
Key events this week include CPI data June 10 and earnings from Oracle and Chewy.

articles.title: Apple WWDC: Gemini-Powered Siri Unveiled in $1B/Year Google Deal; Tim Cook's Last Keynote
bullets: Apple's new Siri runs on a custom Google Gemini model under a $1 billion per year licensing deal.
iOS 27 Extensions let users choose ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude as their default AI assistant.
CEO Tim Cook delivered his final WWDC keynote; John Ternus takes over as CEO on September 1.

articles.title: SpaceX IPO Pricing Set for June 11 at $1.75 Trillion Valuation — Largest IPO in History
bullets: SpaceX targets $75 billion in proceeds at a $1.75 trillion valuation when it prices on June 11.
Trading opens on Nasdaq under ticker SPCX on Friday June 12; book is reportedly oversubscribed.
Thirty percent of the float is allocated to retail investors through Robinhood, Fidelity, and Schwab.

articles.title: Anthropic Files Confidential IPO at $965 Billion Valuation; $44B Annualized Revenue
bullets: Anthropic submitted a confidential IPO filing on June 1 at a $965 billion valuation.
The company reports $44 billion in annualized revenue and is on track for its first operating profit.
IPO proceeds will help fund Anthropic's $1.25 billion per month SpaceX compute bill.

articles.title: Campbell Soup Reports Q3 Earnings Before Bell; Smucker and Casey's Up Next
bullets: Campbell's (CPB) is reporting quarterly earnings before the market opens on Monday June 8.
J.M. Smucker (SJM) and Casey's General Stores (CASY) report earnings on Tuesday June 9.
Chewy (CHWY) and Oracle report Wednesday June 10, the same day May CPI data drops.

articles.title: Microsoft Foundry Reaches 11,000+ AI Models; Claude Comes to Excel Agent Mode
bullets: Microsoft Build 2026 expanded the Foundry model catalog to over 11,000 models from all major labs.
Claude Opus 4.8 is now available in Foundry; Claude also debuts in Excel Agent Mode for 750M users.
Microsoft's own MAI-Thinking-1 is claimed to match Claude Opus 4.6 on coding benchmarks.

articles.title: Apple WWDC 2026: Claude Becomes an iPhone Option via iOS 27 Extensions
bullets: iOS 27 introduces AI Extensions letting users choose ChatGPT, Google Gemini, or Claude as their assistant.
Gemini is the default; each model has a distinct voice so users know which AI responded.
Anthropic gains access to 2.2 billion active Apple devices — potentially doubling its user base.

articles.title: Claude AI Surges 306% in One Quarter — Now 8.2% of Global Chatbot Web Traffic
bullets: Claude's web visits grew from 203 million in January to 824 million in April 2026, up 306%.
ChatGPT still leads at 54.7% of global chatbot web visits; Gemini is second at 27.4%.
In the US specifically, Claude holds 12.5% web-visit market share, ahead of its global average.

articles.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins August 2 — 55 Days Away for High-Risk AI Systems
bullets: The EU AI Act's high-risk system requirements take full effect August 2, 2026.
Fines reach up to 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover for the most serious violations.
Models trained on more than 10^25 FLOPs — including GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.8 — face extra obligations.

articles.title: Colorado AI Act Takes Effect June 30 — First Real US AI Enforcement Deadline
bullets: The Colorado Consumer Protections for AI Act takes effect June 30, covering high-risk AI in 6 sectors.
Requirements include risk management programs, annual impact assessments, and user appeals rights.
A proposed federal preemption bill has not passed; the June 30 deadline is legally active.

articles.title: Microsoft Majorana 2 Quantum Chip Is 1,000x More Reliable Than Its Predecessor
bullets: Microsoft announced Majorana 2, a quantum chip 1,000 times more reliable than the original Majorana.
The chip uses topological qubits, a fundamentally different approach from Google and IBM.
Microsoft says the milestone brings it within striking distance of a commercially useful quantum computer.
